text.tspan: Philip B. Aßmann
, Ibrahim A. Tunc
, Martin Paul Nawrot
Computational Systems Neuroscience, Institute of Zoology, University of Cologne, Germany
Activity [a.u]
Acknowledgment
This research is supported by the German Research Foundation (grant no. 365082554) within the Research Unit Structure,
Plasticity and Behavioral Function of the Drosophila Mushroom Body' (FOR 2705).
References
[1] Manoim, Julia E., et al."Lateral axonal modulation is required for stimulus-specific olfactory
conditioning in Drosophila." Current Biology 32.20 (2022): 4438-4450.
[2] Stahl, Aaron, et al. "Associative learning drives longitudinally graded presynaptic plasticity
of neurotransmitter release along axonal compartments." Elife 11 (2022): e76712.
[3] Bilz, Florian, et al. "Visualization of a distributed synaptic memory code in the Drosophila brain."Neuron 106.6 (2020)
[4] Turner, Glenn C., Maxim Bazhenov, and Gilles Laurent. "Olfactory representations by
Drosophila mushroom body neurons." journal of neurophysiology 99.2 (2008): 734-746.
Model Architecture
Simulation Paradigm
• Weaker DAN activity with each consecutive shock
• b: Calcium response strength to an incoming spike
• The distribution of KC odor responses is tuned to [4]
•Mushroom body (MB): center for learning and memory in insects
•Kenyon cells (KC): Neurons of the MB,
with their dendrites in the calyx and axons in the lobes
•KC axons synapse with dopaminergic neurons (DANs)
and mushroom body output neurons (MBONs) in boutons
•Effects of aversive conditioning in γ2 & γ3 compartments:
=> Reduced acetylcholine release to MBONs
=> Bidirectional change of odor-evoked calcium responses in KC boutons

tspan.tspan: Hypothesis: Dopaminergic modulation of boutons & lateral inhibition

text.tspan: => Coincident DAN & bouton activity reduces boutonal calcium response
=> Modulation is proportional to bouton and DAN response magnitudes
=> Reduction in strong responding boutons,
increase in weakly responding ones due to disinhibition
Introduction
Acetylcholine Release
Calcium Activity
Our model conceptually replicates the experimental findings:
• Significant reduction in mean bouton activity due to aversive conditioning
• Bidirectional modulation of bouton activity
=> Reduction in strong responding boutons
=> Increase in weak responding boutons
Our model provides a mechanistic framework for these processes
• Model the complex microcircuit observed in the mushroom body
=>Connectivity between MBONs, DANs and KC boutons
• Impact of independent bouton modulation
on the representation of various odors
• Transformation of odor representations between various MB compartments
=> Multiplexed odor - valence map ?
Future directions:
Simulation Results
Qualitative match between experimental and model results:
• Significant odor response reduction in the conditioned compartment
• Bidirectional modulation of bouton activity depends on the initial odor
response magnitude
• More uniform bouton responses within the same compartment post conditioning
